title: Unite secures inflation beating pay rise for ICTS screeners at Glasgow airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Unite said it had secured a new pay deal for around 50 ICTS hold baggage screeners at Glasgow Airport. The agreement, backdated to October, gives the workers a 7 per cent increase in basic pay and also raises overtime rates. Unite said the staff carry out x-ray screening of hold baggage, document checks and work on the baggage system.

sections.heading: Recent changes
sections.content: The union said the latest settlement follows a 9.8 per cent increase for the same group last year, which was backdated to October 2024. It said the agreement adds to several years of pay and allowance improvements for ICTS workers at the airport.

sections.heading: Campaign context
sections.content: Unite linked the deal to its 'Runway To Success' campaign, launched in May 2024 after the Covid pandemic's impact on aviation. The campaign aims to restore jobs, pay and conditions removed during Covid, including sick pay, holidays and shift premiums, and to push standards beyond legal minimums at Scottish airports.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Unite secures inflation beating pay rise for ICTS screeners at Glasgow airport
